sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Clicking on the trail segment at (11,48) caused 25 cells to change. Looking at the image, I can see that the white cross/star moved to the position where I clicked (11,48), which is on the trail. The orange-bordered magenta is still at the junction.
So clicking on a trail segment moves the white marker to that position. This is similar to clicking on a magenta object - it moves white to that position.
Let me continue exploring. I'll click on the next trail segment to see if white moves along the trail.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after clicking trail segment
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("White objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        center_row = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        center_col = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  ~({center_row},{center_col}) pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check for white cross/star
print("\nLight gray (w) objects near trail:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'w':
        center_row = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        center_col = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        if n['pixels'] <= 5:
            print(f"  ~({center_row},{center_col}) pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

6. (59,27) - end of trail
7. (56,27) - back one step
Then repeat 3 times total.
So the pattern is: click from start to end of trail, then back one step. Repeat 3 times.
In level 2, I have two trails. Maybe I need to do the same thing on both trails?
